name: The Sacred Geometry, Architect of Perfect Forms
description:
The Sacred Geometry, Architect of Perfect Forms

*{Lore The Sacred Geometry teaches that perfect forms underlie all seeming chaos: the divine ratios that steer the spiral of galaxies, the symmetry that lattices frost, the measures that turn sound into song. Not merely a calculus but the hidden architecture of the world, it makes artists of order from those who serve it. Devotees do not count to chill the heart; they weigh and tune until beauty and structure converge, revealing that disorder is often only unrecognized design waiting to be drawn true.

This philosophy finds its purest expression in the game of Kon'reh --- the living geometry played on an eight-pointed diamond board, where Apexes, Sanctums, and the Cross teach the dance of form and consequence. Yet the Geometry itself is not the game; the game is a mirror of the Geometry. The Ykrul favor Kon'reh as a tool for oath-geometry and battle-field prediction, reading the angles of feuds and the arcs of raids. The Aelinnel, by contrast, use it to navigate thresholds, courtesy, and the hidden patterns of fae bargains --- they count steps not to win, but to avoid stepping where the Green Gate listens. To master Kon'reh is to glimpse the Sacred Geometry in motion; to serve the Geometry is to play the game with the world itself, whether by the steppe fire or the hawthorn arch.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, the Sacred Geometry is called the Architect's Hand. In Aeler, True-Masons carve its ratios into keystones, believing that a properly proportioned arch can outlast any curse. In Ykrul, its principles are taught as "the grammar of honor" --- every Kon'reh move is a lesson in sacrifice and exit. In Thepyrgos, its disciples are geometers who measure the city's staircases to keep the Hollow from noticing a missing step. Wherever a space is built, measured, or bounded, the Sacred Geometry is already present, waiting for someone to read its lines.

quote "In the drift of atoms, the sweep of planets, and the curve of a lover's smile, the same ratios ring. Learn them, and you may conduct the world's quiet music." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Perfect Proportion: building and reading ideal ratios and harmonious relationships Pattern Recognition: discerning the structures that govern complex phenomena Harmonic Resonance: working with natural frequencies and number-made consonance Form Architecture: imposing clear structure on tangled situations Kon'reh Discipline: the game as meditation, ritual, and tactical mirror itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to the Sacred Geometry does not bind a familiar of flesh and blood. Their Thiasos is a living embodiment of perfect form: a small brass beetle whose wing-cases are etched with golden-ratio spirals, a mechanical hummingbird that hovers in mathematically precise arcs, a chameleon whose skin shifts in symmetrical patterns, a compass spider (a tiny arachnid that builds webs in perfect hexagons), or a sentient set of Kon'reh pieces that clack together to spell out advice. This creature requires no food, but it must be allowed to trace geometric figures (real or imagined) each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (its patterns grow erratic, imposing --1 die on all Lore and Investigation rolls involving measurement or analysis until it is recalibrated).

The Codex of the Sacred Geometry is never a normal book. It may be a set of brass stencils of Platonic solids, a roll of vellum covered in interlaced tessellations, a slate tablet on which proofs can be drawn and erased, the inside of a Kon'reh board, or a set of crystal lenses that project perfect geometric shapes when held to the light. Because the Geometry's truths are relationships, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week redrawing or re-proving at least one theorem, or the Rites stored there become tangled (treat as Compromised until re-proved).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Sacred Geometry may touch a held item (weapon, shield, or tool) and inscribe a mental proportion upon it. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Craft or Investigation rolls involving structures, patterns, or measurements, and the first time each scene the bearer would be disoriented (by fog, maze, or supernatural confusion), they may ignore it --- the Geometry holds true. After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Sacred Geometry.
summary: The Sacred Geometry, Architect of Perfect Forms *{Lore The Sacred Geometry teaches that perfect forms underlie all seeming chaos: the divine ratios that steer the spiral of galaxies, the symmetry that lattices frost, the measures that turn sound into song.

rites.name: Rite of the Harmonic Alignment
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A tool calibrated to pristine ratios (e.g., golden ratio gauge, well-cut compass arc, a Kon'reh piece set to true measure). Effect: Align to natural harmonics. Gain +1 die to actions involving balance, proportion, cadence, or keeping true time. Push It: The resonance sharpens to a faultless edge: gain +2 dice instead, but mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as nearby imperfect forms momentarily warp. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Align to natural harmonics. Gain +1 die to actions involving balance, proportion, cadence, or keeping true time. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Perfect Structure
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A consecrated compass and straightedge, inked to tolerance. Effect: Impose right form on a chaotic or damaged structure (scaffold, plan, treaty, formation). Treat one structural obstacle as one step easier; gain +1 die to build/repair/spatial-reason rolls. Push It: Perfection hardens: for the scene the structure gains unnatural resilience, but order draws opposition; mark 1 SB (Clubs). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Impose right form on a chaotic or damaged structure (scaffold, plan, treaty, formation). Treat one structural obstacle as one step easier; gain +1 die to build/repair/spatial-reason rolls. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Resonant Field
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A floor or air traced with precise tessellations and nodes (or a Kon'reh board laid out as a focus). Effect: Establish a harmonic field. Allies gain +1 die to precise, coordinated, or team actions; disruptive acts suffer --1 die. Push It: The field self-sustains and begins to spread beyond intent; mark +1 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Establish a harmonic field. Allies gain +1 die to precise, coordinated, or team actions; disruptive acts suffer --1 die. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Pattern's Heart
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Self; Yes. Tags: , Materials: A solved proof, a perfectly symmetrical token, or a completed Kon'reh puzzle. Effect: Perceive the governing pattern of a complex situation (supply chain, intrigue, weather, battle). Gain +2 dice to prediction/strategy/systemic-understanding and ask two questions about how the pattern drives the present. Push It: Clarity cuts: gain the insight, but for this scene suffer --1 die to intuitive or purely emotional responses; mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Perceive the governing pattern of a complex situation (supply chain, intrigue, weather, battle). Gain +2 dice to prediction/strategy/systemic-understanding and ask two questions about how the pattern drives the present. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Immutable Design
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A space gridded for measure and set with true ratios (like a Kon'reh court). Effect: For the scene, rewrite local behavior by perfect principle. Choose one: gravity follows geometric progression; time flows by harmonic ratios; space conforms to exact symmetry. Push It: Make the design persist, but elsewhere thins and wobbles: mark +2 Obligation and begin Harmonic Dissonance [4]. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For the scene, rewrite local behavior by perfect principle. Choose one: gravity follows geometric progression; time flows by harmonic ratios; space conforms to exact symmetry. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Architect's Vision
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Self; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A ledger of forms and their tuned relations (or a record of Kon'reh victories). Effect: For one session, treat all rolls of pattern recognition, structural analysis, or harmonic application as one step better (Position or Effect, GM's choice). Push It: Sight devours softness: gain the power, but lose ease with imperfection's beauty; mark Harm 1 (Stress). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Session.
rites.effect: For one session, treat all rolls of pattern recognition, structural analysis, or harmonic application as one step better (Position or Effect, GM's choice). \\
